title: Natural Fertiliser for Organic Growers (2 Litres), Approved Organic Plant Food, Concentrate, Chemical Free Safe for Wildlife
features: Approved Organic plant based concentrated liquid fertiliser made from maize.
Chemical free and safe around children, pets and wildlife
Balanced NPK ratio promotes strong healthy growth and increased fruit and vegetable yields. NPK: 3.6-1.5-3.7
The sustainable by-product of a renewable energy plant
Ideal plant food for all flowers, fruits, vegetables, lawns and trees
description: Product Description Grow Greener with Natural Grower Compost and Fertiliser. All products are Certified Organic, BioDynamic and Vegan. Certified Organic by the Soil Association (Date 20.12.21) The Soil Association is a charity that wants to transform the way we eat, farm and care for our natural world. They want to us all to live in a world which is in balance with nature and have a future with good health and a safe climate. They want people to understand and explore the vital relationship between the health of soil, plants, animals and people. The Soil Association certifies over 70% of organic food in the UK, and their organic label is the best way of assuring that the products you buy have been produced to a standard you can trust. Certified BioDynamic by the BioDynamic Association (Date 01.01.2022) BioDynamic and regenerative gardening recognises that soil health and plant growth are ecologically interrelated. It means caring for and nurturing the soil to improve its structure and health, and supporting the micro and macro organisms in the soil. Healthy soil means roots can maximise absorption of nutrients and water. In turn, a healthy plant will feed the microbial life in the soil; which will then support healthy plants, animals, and healthy people. Its time to reverse the damage that has been done and restore the soil to its full potential. Certified Vegan by the Vegan Society (Date 11.02.2022) The Vegan Society has been helping users identify that a product is free from animal ingredients since 1990. According to an Attest consumer survey, over 90% of vegans and vegetarians look for vegan verification, and 85% believe third-party certification is important. Many companies say their gardening products are "Vegan Friendly" or "Vegan" but they do not have any credentials to back this up. This is why certification is so important for people to really know what they are buying and putting on their soil and feeding to their plants.
